title: Minimize access to the approval sub-resource of certificatesigningrequests objects

input_row.title: Minimize access to the approval sub-resource of certificatesigningrequests objects
input_row.description: Users with access to the update the approval sub-resource of CertificateSigningRequests objects can approve new client certificates for the Kubernetes API effectively allowing them to create new high-privileged user accounts. This can allow for privilege escalation to full cluster administrator, depending on users configured in the cluster
input_row.rationale: The ability to update certificate signing requests should be limited.
input_row.audit: Review the users who have access to update the approval sub-resource of CertificateSigningRequests objects in the Kubernetes API.
input_row.remediation: Where possible, remove access to the approval sub-resource of CertificateSigningRequests objects. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/security/rbac-good-practices/#csrs-and- certificate-issuing

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_rbac_csr_approval_subresource_access_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query all ClusterRole and Role objects in the cluster using the Kubernetes API or kubectl:
  - `kubectl get clusterrole -o yaml`
  - `kubectl get role --all-namespaces -o yaml`
- Inspect the `rules` field of each Role/ClusterRole for permissions that grant access to the `certificatesigningrequests/approval` subresource:
  - Look for entries where:
    - `apiGroups` includes `"certificates.k8s.io"`
    - `resources` includes `"certificatesigningrequests/approval"`
    - `verbs` includes `"update"` (or `"*"` which includes `"update"`)
- Enumerate all RoleBindings and ClusterRoleBindings to determine which users, groups, or service accounts are bound to these Roles/ClusterRoles:
  - `kubectl get clusterrolebinding -o yaml`
  - `kubectl get rolebinding --all-namespaces -o yaml`
- For each binding, map the subjects (users, groups, service accounts) that have access to update the `approval` subresource.
- **Pass Criteria:** Only explicitly authorized and minimal set of users/groups/service accounts (ideally dedicated certificate approvers) have `update` access to `certificatesigningrequests/approval`.
- **Fail Criteria:** Any unnecessary or overly broad access (e.g., `system:authenticated`, `system:masters`, or wildcard subjects) is granted `update` on `certificatesigningrequests/approval`.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.11 PDF
